chief_complaint: Pleuritic chest pain 2 days after physical exertion in young female
clinical_reasoning: 28-year-old female with pleuritic chest pain onset 2 days ago after helping a friend move apartments. Pain reproducible on palpation. HR 102 — likely pain-related or anxiety. SpO2 95% is low-normal. Temperature 37.2°C is borderline. No significant chronic medical history. Presentation is most consistent with musculoskeletal chest wall strain with pleuritic component. OCP use noted but without additional risk factors in speech, PE is lower on the differential. YELLOW — urgent but stable, monitor and reassess.
vital_sign_concerns: HR 102 — mild tachycardia, likely pain or anxiety
risk_factors: Prior ED visit for right calf pain
